query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Innocuous', 'Benign, not harmful', 'The reviewer described the tone as innocuous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Innovate', 'To make changes in something established', 'Critics argued that the plan might innovate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Innovation', 'The introduction of new ideas, methods, or devices', 'Her innovation became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Innuendo', 'A derogatory hint or reference to a person or thing', 'Critics argued that the plan might innuendo the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Innumerable', 'Not capable of being counted, indefinitely numerous', 'The passage presents a innumerable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inoculate', 'Immunize someone against', 'Over time, the community learned to inoculate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inoffensive', 'Not offensive', 'What seemed inoffensive at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inopportune', 'Unsuitable for some particular purpose', 'Her inopportune became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inordinate', 'Excessive, unreasonable or inappropriate in magnitude', 'Over time, the community learned to inordinate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inquire', 'To ask (about something)', 'The author hoped the new policy would inquire tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inquisition', 'An investigation or inquiry into the truth of some matter', 'Her inquisition became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inroad', 'An advance into enemy territory, an attempted invasion', 'Scholars noted a striking inroad between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insatiable', 'Impossible to satisfy', '“His insatiable curiosity led him to read every book in the library.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inscribe', 'To write or cut words onto something, to engrave', 'Critics argued that the plan might inscribe the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inscrutable', 'Impossible to understand or interpret', 'The passage presents a inscrutable portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insecure', 'Not secure', 'The reviewer described the tone as insecure, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insensible', 'Unable to be perceived by the senses', 'Over time, the community learned to insensible its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insentient', 'Having no consciousness or animation, not sentient', 'The passage presents a insentient portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inseparable', 'Not able to be separated, bound together permanently', 'Critics argued that the plan might inseparable the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insidious', 'Proceeding in a gradual, harmful way', 'The reviewer described the tone as insidious, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insight', 'A sight or view of the interior of anything, introspection', 'Her insight became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insightful', 'Having deep understanding or perception', '“Her insightful comments added depth to the discussion.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insinuate', 'To make a way for or introduce something by subtle means', 'Over time, the community learned to insinuate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insipid', 'Lacking flavor, interest, or vigor', '“The lecture was insipid, putting the students to sleep.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insistent', 'Standing or resting on something', 'The reviewer described the tone as insistent, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insolence', 'Arrogant conduct, insulting, bold behaviour or attitude', 'Scholars noted a striking insolence between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insolent', 'Showing a rude and arrogant lack of respect', 'The reviewer described the tone as insolent, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insolvent', 'Unable to pay debts owed', 'Over time, the community learned to insolvent its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insomnia', 'A sleeping disorder that is known for inability to sleep', 'Critics argued that the plan might insomnia the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insouciant', 'Free from concern, worry, or anxiety, carefree, nonchalant', 'Scholars noted a striking insouciant between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inspector', 'A person employed to inspect something', 'The author hoped the new policy would inspector tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Inspire', 'Filled with creativity or motivation', '“His inspired speech moved the audience.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Instance', 'To mention as a case or example, to refer to, to cite', 'Over time, the community learned to instance its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Instant', 'An infinitesimal or very short space of time, a moment', 'The essay highlights the instant of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Instigate', 'Bring about; incite', 'The author hoped the new policy would instigate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Instigator', 'A person who intentionally incites or starts something', 'Her instigator became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Instill', 'To cause a quality to become part of someone''s nature', 'Critics argued that the plan might instill the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Institute', 'To begin or initiate something', 'Critics argued that the plan might institute the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Instructive', 'Conveying knowledge, information or instruction', 'The reviewer described the tone as instructive, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insufficient', 'Not sufficient', 'What seemed insufficient at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insular', 'Isolated or having a narrow viewpoint', '“The small town had an insular culture, resistant to change.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insulate', 'Cover or line with material to prevent passage of heat, etc', 'The author hoped the new policy would insulate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insult', 'To treat or speak to insolently, with contemptuous rudeness', 'The author hoped the new policy would insult tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insuperable', 'Impossible to overcome or surpass; insurmountable', 'Over time, the community learned to insuperable its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insuppressible', 'That cannot be suppressed', 'The essay highlights the insuppressible of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insurgence', 'An uprising or rebellion, an insurrection', 'Scholars noted a striking insurgence between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insurgent', 'Rebel or revolutionary', 'What seemed insurgent at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Insurrection', 'An organized opposition to an authority, a rebellion', 'Critics argued that the plan might insurrection the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Intangible', 'Unable to be touched or grasped; not physical', 'Over time, the community learned to intangible its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Integral', 'Essential or necessary for completeness', '“A good education is integral to success.”', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
